summary: DTS Task Assignment UI + Notification Triggers Testing - All P0 features verified. Backend: 17/18 tests passed (1 skipped due to rate limiting). Frontend: All DTS features working correctly.

minor.issue: Rate limiting (429) after rapid requests - expected behavior, test skipped

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/dts/tasks/all - Returns all DTS tasks for admin
Backend: GET /api/founder/operators - Returns list of operators for assignment dropdown
Backend: POST /api/dts/tasks/{id}/status?task_status=quoted - Status update works correctly
Backend: POST /api/dts/tasks/{id}/status with invalid status - Returns 400 error
Backend: POST /api/dts/tasks/{id}/assign - Assigns task to operator successfully
Backend: POST /api/dts/tasks/{id}/assign with invalid operator - Returns 404 error
Backend: GET /api/notifications - Returns notifications with {notifications:[], unread_count:N} format
Backend: GET /api/notifications/unread-count - Returns {unread_count: N}
Backend: POST /api/notifications/{id}/read - Marks notification as read
Backend: POST /api/notifications/read-all - Marks all as read
Backend: GET /api/estates - Returns benefactor estates
Backend: GET /api/documents/{estate_id} - Returns estate documents
Backend: POST /api/dts/tasks/{id}/quote - Quote submission works with line items
Backend: GET /api/admin/users - Founder admin endpoint accessible
Backend: Notification triggers implemented in auth.py (new user signup → founder)
Backend: Notification triggers implemented in beneficiaries.py (invitation accepted → benefactor)
Backend: Notification triggers implemented in documents.py (document upload → beneficiaries)
Frontend: DTS Tab - Shows list of DTS tasks with status badges and assigned operator names
Frontend: DTS Detail View - Shows task details, quote line items, status buttons
Frontend: DTS Assign Operator - Select dropdown with operator list (data-testid='dts-assign-select')
Frontend: DTS Status Update - 6 status buttons (submitted/quoted/approved/ready/executed/destroyed)
Frontend: DTS Assigned Operator Badge - Green badge showing operator name on task cards
Frontend: Operators Tab - Shows manager/worker hierarchy with badges
Frontend: Notifications sidebar - Accessible in founder dashboard
critical_code_review_comments: DTS assignment endpoint (POST /api/dts/tasks/{id}/assign) correctly validates operator role and sends notification
Status update endpoint uses query param 'task_status' (not body) - correctly implemented
Notification service (services/notifications.py) provides centralized notify namespace for all notification types
Registration notification trigger in auth.py uses asyncio.create_task(notify.founder(...)) - async fire-and-forget
Invitation acceptance notification in beneficiaries.py correctly notifies benefactor when invitation is accepted
Document upload notification in documents.py notifies all linked beneficiaries with document category label

seed_data_creation: Used existing test DTS tasks: TEST_DTS_Notification_1772992649, TEST UI Delete Task
context_for_next_testing_agent: DTS task assignment UI fully implemented. DTSTab fetches operators via GET /api/founder/operators on mount. Assign Operator section only shows for admin and manager roles. Uses Select component from shadcn/ui with data-testid='dts-assign-select'. Status API format: POST /api/dts/tasks/{id}/status?task_status=quoted (query param, not body). Rate limiting on auth after ~10 rapid logins - add delays between auth tests.

dts_assignment_ui: Assign Operator section with Select dropdown
Currently assigned to: displays operator name with green checkmark
Assignment triggers operator notification via notify.operator()
dts_status_updates: 6 status buttons: submitted, quoted, approved, ready, executed, destroyed
Current status highlighted with gold background
dts_list_view: Task cards show assigned operator name in green badge
notification_triggers: New user registration → founder notification (auth.py line 662-669)
operators_tab: Shows OPERATIONS MANAGERS section with count
Shows OPERATIONS WORKERS section with count
Manager badge in cyan/teal, Worker badge in green
